decision.recommended_next_action: Package the synthetic and real-log harnesses as a reusable cluster-balanced evaluator and apply them to the next real verifier-log dataset with broader task domains.
decision.stop_reason: The project now has both synthetic MVP evidence and a 1,000-example real-log validation showing a cluster-balanced ranking shift and an actionable long-range dependency failure cluster.

recommended_next_action: Package the synthetic and real-log harnesses as a reusable cluster-balanced evaluator and apply them to the next real verifier-log dataset with broader task domains.
run_notes_tail: Ranking comparison excluding obvious oracle/control methods (`full_context_oracle`, `final_keyword_retriever_top1`):
- `tail_512` rises above `boundary_probe_2048_lookahead256` under cluster balancing despite lower aggregate accuracy (aggregate: 0.600 vs 0.700; cluster-balanced: 0.750 vs 0.688).

- The shift is interpretable: `tail_512` is perfect on near/mid/long-range dependency clusters but fails in-window evidence, while `boundary_probe_2048_lookahead256` is smoother but substantially weaker on long-range dependency.

The real-log pass supports the same core mechanism outside the synthetic MVP: cluster-balanced evaluation changes at least one candidate system ranking and exposes an actionable long-range dependency weakness that aggregate scoring partly hides. Evidence is now stronger than the first pass because it uses existing real verifier/model-output artifacts with 1,000 examples and 18,000 prediction rows, though the validation is still concentrated in a long-context/evidence-position benchmark rather than all four originally proposed domains.
